stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: New Hungary PM signals shift away from Kremlin after massive drone attack on Ukraine
stories.summary: Péter Magyar says his government has summoned Russian ambassador over drone attack near Hungary’s border to ask when Vladimir Putin plans ‘to finally end this bloody war’. What we know on day 1,541 Hungarian prime minister Péter Magyar says his new government has summoned the Russian ambassador over a massive drone attack near Hungary’s border in a significant shift from his predecessor’s friendly relations with Moscow . “The Hungarian government strongly condemns the Russian attack on Transcarp

stories.title: EU Commission’s structure leaves Brussels green campaigners dazed and confused
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — Ursula von der Leyen’s European Commission structure is proving a headache for green and equality advocates, who say industry has a smoother path to lobbying the institution. With many portfolios split among commissioners, it’s increasingly difficult to reach the right person to try to shape legislation, the advocates told POLITICO’s EU Influence newsletter . Lobbyists of all kinds are also concerned about the top-down nature of the von der Leyen Commission, where po

There are also 514 crew members. Ambassador Cruise Line also confirmed that a 92-year-old man died on board earlier this week, but he did not report any symptoms at the time and the cause of his death is yet to be established. stories.title: Nebraska Democratic Senate primary winner says she’ll drop out to support independent in general election
stories.summary: Cindy Burbank, who bested an alleged Republican plant, plans to step aside for Dan Osborn in general election Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email A Democratic challenger who said she intends to drop out of November’s race for the US Senate in Nebraska to clear the way for an independent candidate has won the state’s Democratic primary. Cindy Burbank ran against William Forbes, who Democrats contended was a Republican plant in the race, with the intent to drop out if she won. Forbes

stories.title: The Guardian view on the king’s speech: an agenda for a government that lacks conviction | Editorial
stories.summary: Keir Starmer’s programme is fatally limited by the timidity of an election manifesto that shied away from hard arguments Ending 14 years of Conservative rule was supposed to bring an end to dysfunctional government. In the speech that launched his 2024 general election campaign , Sir Keir Starmer said that “a vote for Labour is a vote for stability … a vote to stop the chaos”. Less than two years later, Sir Keir’s government looks no sturdier than its predecessors. The prime minister’s chances o

stories.summary: In House committee transcript, commerce secretary denied any further contact with disgraced financier Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email The US commerce secretary, Howard Lutnick, told lawmakers in a closed-door interview earlier this month that he met Jeffrey Epstein only three times and had no “personal or professional relationship” with the disgraced financier, according to a newly released transcript of the meeting. “I unequivocally condemn the conduct attributed to Jeffrey Ep

stories.title: Hungarian Foreign Minister summons Russian ambassador over drone attack on Transcarpathia
stories.summary: The Hungarian government has strongly condemned Russia's attack against the area which is home to Ukraine's ethnic Hungarian minority.

stories.title: Szabó Andrea: Tévhit, hogy a 40 éves korosztálynál volt a választóvonal a Fidesz és a Tisza támogatottságában
stories.summary: Orbán Viktor és Orbán Balázs is erről beszélt a vereség után, de a politológus szerint két több ezer fős kutatás egészen más eredményt mutat.

stories.title: A lengyel külügy jegyzékben érdeklődik a magyar kormánynál az Amerikába menekült Zbigniew Ziobróról
stories.summary: A volt lengyel igazságügyi miniszter saját állítása szerint egy magyar okmánnyal menekült az Egyesült Államokba.

stories.title: Belga klasszis győzött a magyar körverseny pusztai szakaszán
stories.summary: Tim Merlier győzött a Tour de Hongrie békéscsabai befutóján. A legjobb magyar bringás, Kárpáti Bálint lett a 10. helyével.

stories.title: Nagy Márton januárban eltöltött egy éjszakát Izraelben, most kiderült, hogy 611 ezer forintért
stories.summary: Igaz, nem egyedül ment, a protokollfőnökének is aludnia kellett valahol, így kettejük szállása együtt került ennyibe.

stories.title: Orbán Anita berendelte az orosz nagykövetet a Kárpátalját ért dróntámadás miatt
stories.summary: Magyar Péter azt mondta, a magyar kormány mélységesen elítéli az orosz támadást Kárpátalja ellen.

stories.title: Kik ültek a miniszterek mellett az ópusztaszeri kormányülésen?
stories.summary: Az új miniszterek mellett már feltűntek közvetlen munkatársaik, és a kirakott névtáblák is segítettek az azonosításban.

stories.title: Tóth Pétert, a Tisza kampányfőnökét nevezik ki a kormány nemzetbiztonsági főtanácsadójává
stories.summary: Magyar Péter a szerdai sajtótájékoztatón hosszan beszélt arról, miért alkalmas szerinte Tóth erre a feladatra.

stories.title: Átvilágítás, pénzügyi stop – itt vannak a kormány első döntései
stories.summary: Az elmúlt tizenhat év után szokatlan formában és helyszínen Magyar Péter jelentette be kormánya első intézkedéseit. Volt kampányfőnöke, Tóth Péter lesz a miniszterelnök nemzetbiztonsági főtanácsadója, Orbán Anita bekérette az orosz nagykövetet, és már az első kormányülésen 17 határozatról tárgyalnak. Mutatjuk a részleteket.

stories.title: A kormány hivatalos honlapján már Tisza-kormány van, a miniszterelnök oldala továbbra is Orbáné
stories.summary: Kedd este még az előző kormány és kormányfő volt a kormány honlapján, pedig Magyar Péter már szombaton is miniszterelnök volt.

stories.title: Az ukrán kormány kidolgozott egy tervet a nemzeti kisebbségek jogi helyzetének rendezésére
stories.summary: Az ukrán miniszterelnök-helyettes szerint a magyarok érdekeit figyelembe véve dolgozták ki a részleteket.

stories.title: A csütörtöki közlönyben jelennek majd meg a Magyar-kormány első kormányülésén született döntések
stories.summary: Szondi Vanda kormányszóvivő szerint szerdán este 11-kor ért véget az első kormányülés.

stories.title: Magyar Péter szerint már akár holnapra, holnaputánra meglehet a kegyelmi ügy egyik dossziéja
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök azt mondta, nem számít arra, hogy „Novák Katalin leírta, hogy azért adtam kegyelmet, mert Lévai Anikó erre kért”.

stories.title: Magyar vows to bring EU funds home and release clemency scandal files
stories.summary: The Hungarian PM spoke about the new government's most urgent priorities after their first official cabinet meeting.

stories.title: Shaquille O’Neal júliusban Budapesten lép fel
stories.summary: Az amerikai kosárlabdázó a Subtronics nevű előadóval fog DJ-zni.

stories.title: Feltárná a necces ügyeket és a klímakárok súlyos összegeivel érvelne Gajdos László helyettes államtitkára
stories.summary: A WWF Magyarország igazgatói posztjáról érkező Sipos Katalin a Hvg.hu-nak beszélt az elképzeléseiről.

stories.title: Zelenszkij az orosz dróntámadás után Magyar Péternek: Köszönöm együttérzését és határozott kiállását
stories.summary: Az ukrán elnök szerint Oroszország ismét bizonyította, hogy egész Európa számára fenyegetést jelent.

stories.title: Száz éve a spanyolnátha mellett pusztított egy titokzatos álomkór is, aminek a mai napig nem tudják az okát
stories.summary: Egyes becslések szerint 1917 és 1925 között világszerte egymillió embert érintett, a betegek fele bele is halt a különös letargiával és egyéb neurológiai problémákkal járó betegségbe.

stories.title: Szinte már hagyomány az elszálló költségvetés a választási évben, de mi jöhet ezután?
stories.summary: Hatalmas hiánnyal küzd az idei költségvetés a választási év közepén. Bár a régióban nem szükségszerű, Magyarországon rendszeresen ugyanaz a minta rajzolódik ki a választási években.

stories.title: A magyar edző, aki leült csapata sztárjaival inni és kártyázni, elnyerte a pénzüket, aztán megnyerték a BEK-et kétszer
stories.summary: Május 30-án a sorozat történetében először rendezheti meg Budapest a Bajnokok Ligája döntőjét, a Puskás Arénában ünnepelhet Európa legjobb futballcsapata. A fináléig mindennap visszatekintünk a döntők egy-egy nagy pillanatára, egyéniségére, érdekességére. BL-sztorik, nyolcadik rész.

stories.title: Bérlakásépítésre költené az uniós pénzek egy részét az új kormány
stories.summary: Az előző kormány lakáspolitikája az új miniszterek szerint túlságosan egysíkú volt, a lakástulajdonlás mellett a más élethelyzetben is releváns lakhatási megoldásokat is támogatnák.

stories.title: Bódis Kriszta szerint nincs semmiféle titok akörül, hogy miért nem lett szociális miniszter
stories.summary: Az író, dokumentumfilmes a Facebook-oldalán értekezett arról, mi lesz a feladata, és mivel foglalkozik most.

stories.title: Nem szoktunk hozzá, hogy egy normális országban élünk, de próbáljuk meg, mert itt van most rá a lehetőség
stories.summary: Pósfai Mihály, a Magyar Tudományos Akadémia újonnan megválasztott elnöke interjúnkban azt mondta, számára nincs jelentősége annak, hogy rokona a belügyminiszter, családon belül nem szoktak protekciózni. Nem tartja problémának, ha az MTA tagjai politikai állásfoglalást tesznek, és a kiválóságokon kívül szeretné támogatni a kutatói derékhadat is.

stories.title: Az RTL Magyarország megszerezte a Bajnokok Ligája teljes csomagjának közvetítési jogait
stories.summary: 2031-ig csak itt lehet majd nézni a meccseket.

stories.title: Több mint ezer utas rekedt egy óceánjárón egy francia kikötőben, egy ember meghalt emésztőrendszeri vírus terjedése miatt
stories.summary: A francia hatóságok senkit sem engedtek leszállni a Bordeaux-ban kikötött hajóról, amin eddig ötven betegről tudni.

stories.title: Jó oka volt rá Magyar Péternek, hogy megdöntötte a kormányalakítás rekordját
stories.summary: Mindössze 740 órával az április 12-i választás urnazárása után szimbolikus helyszínen, Ópusztaszeren tartják az első kormányülést. A feszes menetrend ellenére volt egy átmeneti időszak, amikor Magyar Péter miniszterelnökként még a leköszönő kormány tagjainak adott utasítást. Gyors döntésekre és sűrű nyárra készülnek.

stories.title: Egy autókereskedőtől is kedvezményt kért magának a korrupcióval gyanúsított, volt fideszes képviselő
stories.summary: Bóna Zoltánt nemcsak azzal gyanúsítják, hogy papíron a halászteleki polgármester 70 éves anyját foglalkoztatta maga mellett az országgyűlési frakcióban.

stories.title: Orbán Balázs: Tegyünk úgy, mintha egy normális, civilizált országban lennénk
stories.summary: A Patrióta műsorában beszélt az általa vezetett kampány kudarcáról, a Fidesz jövőjéről és a Magyar Péter által bemutatott minisztériumokról.

stories.title: Orbán Anita elítélte a Kárpátalját elérő orosz dróntámadást, a kormányülésen is szó lesz róla
stories.summary: A külügyminiszter azt mondta, folyamatosan kapcsolatban vannak az ottani főkonzullal, és be fognak számolni a fejleményekről.

stories.title: Magyar Péter: Orbán Anita berendelte az orosz nagykövetet – sajtótájékoztató és elemzés
stories.summary: Magyar Péter nehéz helyzetbe hozta a távozó fideszes minisztereket, heti két kormányülésre készülnek, a Tisza kampányfőnöke nemzetbiztonsági főtanácsadó lesz. Hanula Zsolt és Király András segítségével élőben közvetítettük, majd elemeztük a Tisza-kormány első ülése utáni ópusztaszeri sajtótájékoztatót az elmúlt napok történéseinek összefoglalása mellett.

stories.title: Magyar Péter: Rogán Antal, Lázár János és az országot tönkretevő miniszterek nem fogják megkapni a végkielégítésüket
stories.summary: Nagyon-nagyon rossz állapotban veszi át a Tisza-kormány az országot Magyar Péter szerint. Megvárják, hogy lemondanak-e május végéig a közjogi méltóságok, de ha nem, akkor alkotmánymódosítással mozdítják el őket. A három új kormányszóvivő partneri viszonyt ajánlott a sajtónak. Magyar Péter bejelentette a kormány első intézkedéseit. Percről percre tudósításunk.

stories.title: EU approves sanctions on Israeli settlers after Hungarian backing
stories.summary: The sanctions — which include asset freezes and travel bans and will also target Hamas members — come amid growing international criticism of Israel’s settlement expansion and escalating violence in the occupied territories.

stories.title: Spain’s alleged misuse of post-Covid funds sparks fury in frugal northern EU
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — Spain’s alleged misuse of the EU’s post-Covid funds to pay government pensions is triggering outrage in Germany and the Netherlands and reigniting criticism of joint EU debt. The European Commission has backed Spain in the escalating row over whether Madrid misspent up to €10 billion in EU recovery funds — intended to finance green and digital projects to enhance economic recovery — to plug a hole in its pensions system and finance social expenditure. But the possible misspending, whi

stories.title: Trump’s disappearing China hawks
stories.summary: As the world’s two most powerful leaders prepare to meet, the usual chorus of Republican warnings about the perils of dealing with Beijing has disappeared. The silence is a culmination of President Donald Trump’s stifling of GOP orthodoxy on China, which has washed away years of hawkish policy on everything from tech to defense. He has green-lighted the sale of advanced AI chips to Beijing, even as Congress warned about espionage risks. He signed off on a deal to allow Chinese-founded TikTok to

stories.title: The POLITICO Poll: Starmer has failed to deliver the ‘change’ he promised
stories.summary: LONDON — Keir Starmer has failed so badly in his first two years as prime minister that his Labour Party is now widely seen as unelectable, no matter who ends up leading it. That is the brutal assessment from The POLITICO Poll run by Public First, which questioned more than 2,000 people across the U.K. after last week’s local elections. With the British prime minister clinging onto his job by a thread, only 6 percent of respondents said Labour had changed the country for the better as much as th

stories.summary: Lu Jianwang was accused of operating a ‘secret police station’ in Manhattan’s Chinatown at the behest of Beijing A New York man was found guilty on Wednesday of acting as an unregistered agent of the Chinese government after he was accused of operating a “secret police station” on behalf of Beijing in Manhattan’s Chinatown neighborhood. stories.title: Judge overturns US sanctions on UN official who called for war crimes prosecutions over Gaza
stories.summary: A federal judge has blocked the Trump administration’s sanctions against a United Nations official who has faced accusations of antisemitism over her calls for war crimes charges against Israeli officials over their actions in Gaza. Secretary of State Marco Rubio imposed the sanctions on Francesca Albanese, the United Nations special rapporteur for Palestinian human rights, last July under an executive order President Donald Trump signed authorizing such actions against people “directly engaged”

stories.title: EU proposes end to ‘five tabs, three apps and a prayer’ for cross-border train bookings
stories.summary: New rules would enable single-ticket bookings across multiple rail operators throughout Europe Cross-border train journeys through several European countries are the stuff of many a holidaymaker’s dreams. But the reality of trying to buy the tickets, navigating multiple websites without knowing who can help if a connection is missed, can prove less than relaxing. As one MEP puts it, it can often require “five tabs, three apps and a prayer”. Continue reading...

stories.title: Israel’s ruling coalition proposes early elections amid ultra-Orthodox anger at Netanyahu

stories.title: Gunshots fired in Philippines senate in standoff with senator wanted by ICC
stories.summary: Ronald dela Rosa, accused of crimes against humanity by international criminal court, has holed up in building to evade arrest Gunshots have been fired in the Philippines senate as a senator who is wanted by the international criminal court (ICC) remained holed up in the building to evade arrest. Ronald dela Rosa, a Philippine senator accused of crimes against humanity for his role in overseeing the former president Rodrigo Duterte’s “war on drugs”, has spent two nights in the country’s senate i

stories.title: Russia unleashes fatal barrage of drones at Ukraine
stories.summary: At least six people died in Ukraine after Russia launched a daytime attack of 800 drones of drones as far as western regions bordering NATO. Zelenskyy said it was one of the longest and largest attacks of the war.

stories.summary: The foreign minister says the "distressed" Ghanaians registered with the embassy in Pretoria to be evacuated.

stories.summary: An Israeli commission's two-year investigation concludes Hamas systematically used sexual violence on 7 October and against hostages it held in Gaza, laying groundwork for prosecution.

stories.summary: Senator Ronald dela Rosa, a former police chief, enforced former President Rodrigo Duterte's deadly drug war.

stories.title: Kevin Warsh confirmed as Fed chair, ushering in new era for central bank
stories.summary: The Senate on Wednesday confirmed Kevin Warsh as the next chair of the Federal Reserve by a 54-45 vote, giving the former Wall Street banker a shot at overhauling an institution that he has long criticized. Warsh got the support of only a single Democrat, Sen.  John Fetterman  of Pennsylvania, presaging a rockier relationship with Congress than his predecessor, outgoing Chair Jerome Powell, enjoyed. He enters the job at a fraught moment for the central bank, with President Donald Trump

stories.title: Macron allies deny Brigitte slapped French president over texts with Iranian actress
stories.summary: PARIS — Team Brigitte is slapping back against allegations her relationship isn’t perfect. A person close to the First Lady, granted anonymity to discuss a sensitive matter, told POLITICO that Brigitte Macron “has categorically denied” allegations that her dust-up with her husband last year caught on camera was due to revelations of his purported relationship with an actress. The claim was made in a new book about the pair, “An (almost) perfect couple,” by Paris Match journalist Florian Ta

stories.title: Iran war is fueling China’s clean energy surge ahead of Trump-Xi talks
stories.summary: President Donald Trump has said that energy is one of the key topics he’ll raise in an otherwise trade-focused trip to Beijing that starts today – but the trip isn’t likely to address an uncomfortable truth: the Iran war may be among the best clean energy salesmen China’s had. Indeed, the 10 weeks of global oil market convulsions caused by the U.S. war against Iran has swept into China’s arms countries looking to replace lost crude shipments with clean energy tech. If the Iran war’s impact on en

stories.title: Vance jokes he and Rubio are ‘Apprentice’ candidates to succeed Trump
stories.summary: Vice President JD Vance brushed aside President Donald Trump’s speculation about his and Secretary of State Marco Rubio’s potential presidential campaign while likening Trump’s fascination with the heir to GOP leadership to “The Apprentice.” Vance told reporters at a press conference on Wednesday that he isn’t thinking about 2028, while calling Rubio a “very, very dear friend” and insisting he’s concentrating on his role in the Trump administration. But the president appears invested in the poss

stories.title: Andy Burnham’s backers press Labour’s ruling body for long leadership election timetable
stories.summary: LONDON — Backers of Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham are pressing the Labour Party’s ruling body to agree an extended leadership election timetable, three people with knowledge of the conversations have told POLITICO — a move that would give him a chance to return to Parliament and become eligible to run for prime minister. The party’s national executive committee, or NEC, is expected to meet in the coming days to agree on a timeline for a leadership election if Health Secretary Wes Streeti

stories.title: Les Simpson: return of Québécois show spares viewers from ‘European French’
stories.summary: Beloved animated series will return for 36th season in the fall after telecoms giant Bell Media reaches deal with Disney Fans of Les Simpson have a message for anyone who doubted the future of the beloved and long-running Québécois version of the animated satirical show: Mange de la crotte. Les Simpson will return for its 36th season in the fall after telecoms giant Bell Media said it had reached an agreement with Disney for the rights to air and dub the show. The deal caps nearly a year of unce

stories.title: Milka maker milked shoppers over size of chocolate bars, German court rules
stories.summary: Brand owner Mondelēz was accused of reducing weight of Alpine Milk bar from 100g to 90g without significantly altering the packaging Many chocolate lovers consider shrinkflation a serious crime – and they have been vindicated after a German court ruled that the makers of Milka cheated consumers by cutting the bar’s size, while keeping the wrapper the same. The three-week case in a regional court was brought by Hamburg’s consumer protection office. It accused the chocolate brand’s US owner Mondel

stories.title: Benjamin Netanyahu says he made secret trip to UAE at height of Iran war
stories.summary: Emirates’ foreign ministry rejects claims that Netanyahu visited the country describing them as ‘baseless’ Benjamin Netanyahu has claimed he made a secret trip to the United Arab Emirates at the height of the Iran war to meet the president, Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan. “This visit has led to a historic breakthrough in relations between Israel and the UAE,” the Israeli prime minister’s office said on Wednesday night. Continue reading...

stories.title: Valdo Calocane brother tells inquiry he felt powerless over killer’s mental ill health
stories.summary: Elias Calocane says he thought violent messages sent by brother related to suicidal thoughts not harming others The younger brother of Valdo Calocane, who killed three people in an attack in Nottingham, said he felt “powerless” over his sibling’s mental ill health and believed violent messages his brother had sent concerned suicidal thoughts. Valdo Calocane, who was diagnosed with paranoid schizophrenia in 2020, stabbed to death Barnaby Webber and Grace O’Malley-Kumar, both 19, and caretaker Ian

stories.title: Family of British couple jailed in Iran say they have lost contact
stories.summary: Lindsay and Craig Foreman were given 10-year sentences after entering the country on a motorcycling trip The “terrified” family of a British couple jailed for 10 years in Iran on spying charges have said they have lost all contact with them. Lindsay and Craig Foreman, both 53, were arrested in January 2025 while travelling through Iran during an around-the-world trip by motorcycle. Continue reading...

stories.title: Influencer detained in Tennessee over alleged confrontation involving gunfire
stories.summary: Dalton Eatherly, 28, known as Chud the Builder, accused of firing at another man outside courthouse in Clarksville An influencer has been detained in Tennessee following his alleged involvement in a shooting, according to local police. On Wednesday afternoon, Dalton Eatherly, 28 – known online as Chud the Builder – was involved in a confrontation with another man outside the Montgomery county courthouse in Clarksville, Tennessee, during which shots were fired, the office of the district attorney

stories.title: NHS drugs go-ahead offers lifeline to children with rare muscle-wasting disease
stories.summary: Medicines watchdog approves two treatments for patients with spinal muscular atrophy Hundreds of children with a rare muscle-wasting disease will be able to receive two drugs that can improve their survival in a move parents hailed as a “lifeline”. The National Institute for Health and Care Excellence (Nice) has published final draft guidance recommending that any patient who would benefit can have either drug. Continue reading...

stories.title: Typical English roast dinner potentially ‘drenched’ in 102 pesticides, says report
stories.summary: Greenpeace finds cocktail of pesticides including seven banned in EU may have been used on seven categories of vegetables and soft fruit It is a beautiful early summer Sunday afternoon and you have stopped for a pub lunch. A waiter sets down a roast served with carrots, peas, parsnips, potatoes and onion gravy, and then for pudding, strawberries and cream. It feels like the perfect rustic meal to accompany a day in the country. However, a report by Greenpeace, published on Thursday, has found th

stories.title: Housing market in England and Wales weakening due to Iran war, say estate agents
stories.summary: Homebuyers more cautious due to possible mortgage rate rises and higher inflation as sellers sit on properties Fears of higher mortgage rates and rising inflation as a result of the Middle East conflict are leading to a subdued and downbeat housing market, according to estate agents. Demand from potential homebuyers across England and Wales has shown a “noticeable softening” recently, according to a monthly survey of estate agents by the Royal Institution of Chartered Surveyors (RICS). Continue

stories.title: Court rules Coles misled shoppers with its ‘Down Down’ discount campaign
stories.summary: Landmark finding against supermarket giant comes after competition watchdog argued that markdowns did not represent genuine savings Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Coles misled Australian shoppers by promoting fake “Down Down” discounts on everyday grocery products, the federal court has ruled in a landmark decision for the supermarket industry. Justice Michael O’Bryan handed down his judgment on Thursday, delive

stories.title: ‘Stunned and shocked’: Ingrid Horrocks wins top prize at New Zealand’s Ockham awards for her fiction debut
stories.summary: All Her Lives is only the fifth short story collection to win the prestigious NZ$65,000 prize in 58 years First-time fiction writer Ingrid Horrocks has won New Zealand’s richest literary prize for her debut short story collection, All Her Lives . The Wellington-based poet, essayist and memoirist won the prestigious NZ$65,000 (A$53,000, £28,500) Jann Medlicott Acorn prize for fiction at the 2026 Ockham New Zealand book awards on Wednesday night. The book follows nine women across nine different l

stories.title: ‘Blatant disregard for rights’: concern grows over Gabon’s social media clampdown
stories.summary: Activists claim use of laws to curtail internet freedoms part of well-documented history of cracking down on dissent When Gabon’s media regulator indefinitely suspended major social media platforms in February, citing security concerns during anti-government protests, it became the talk of town – literally. Within weeks of the announcement, use of Virtual Private Networks (VPNs) to bypass the restrictions surged in the central African country. When gendarmerie began stopping young men at road ch

stories.title: Bahamas re-elects Progressive Liberal party’s Philip Davis as prime minister
stories.summary: Davis is the country’s first leader to serve a second consecutive term in nearly 30 years The Bahamas prime minister, Philip Davis, and his ruling Progressive Liberal party (PLP) have been re-elected , making him the country’s first leader to serve a second consecutive term in nearly 30 years. “The Bahamian people have spoken, and I receive their verdict with humility and gratitude,” Davis told Reuters. “This victory is a mandate to keep moving the Bahamas forward, to expand opportunity, strengt

stories.title: Russia targets Ukraine with more than 800 drones in deadly daytime assault
stories.summary: Strikes killed at least six people as Moscow and Kyiv trade long-range attacks after brief ceasefire Europe live – latest updates Russia targeted Ukraine with more than 800 drones in a large-scale daytime assault that killed at least six people on Wednesday, hours after a previous deadly barrage. The strikes came as Kyiv and Moscow traded long-range attacks after a brief ceasefire, and despite the latest suggestion from Donald Trump that the war could soon come to an end. Continue reading...

stories.title: France: Cruise ship on lockdown over stomach bug outbreak
stories.summary: A stomach illness outbreak and the death of a man on board prompted authorities in Bordeaux to place a temporary disembarking ban on the ship.

stories.title: Trump's Federal Reserve chair nominee confirmed by Senate
stories.summary: Kevin Warsh is set to replace outgoing Fed chair Jerome Powell, who has been a frequent target of President Trump's criticism.

stories.title: US drops bond requirement for FIFA World Cup ticket holders
stories.summary: Fans from five African World Cup qualifying nations with valid tickets are exempt from paying a hefty bond. But fans from several other countries still face major hurdles to seeing their teams play in the United States.

stories.title: India's Modi cuts size of convoy in austerity move — reports
stories.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has reportedly reduced the number of vehicles in his official convoy. The reports come as Modi is making an austerity appeal to Indians.

stories.title: Iran war: Oil stocks being used up at record pace, IEA warns
stories.summary: The International Energy Agency warned of "rapidly shrinking buffers." Meanwhile, Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi is en route to Delhi for a BRICS gathering which is set to be dominated by the conflict. DW has more.

stories.title: Indians growing anxious as Modi asks them to cut on fuel
stories.summary: With energy prices rising, Prime Minister Narendra Modi has urged Indians to embrace voluntary austerity to protect the economy from the fallout of the war in Iran. But residents say they are already at their limit.

stories.title: Can South Africa's President Ramaphosa avoid impeachment again?
stories.summary: Ramaphosa sidestepped an impeachment inquiry in 2022 when his ANC-dominated parliament blocked it. Now that South Africa's top court has revived the case, can he escape with a parliament accomodating former adversaries?

stories.title: Germany's shortfall in medium-range defense capabilities
stories.summary: The US has announced that it will not be deploying Tomahawk cruise missiles in Germany. Now the German government is looking for alternatives. Could drones be part of the solution?

stories.title: Trade, Iran and Taiwan on the agenda as Trump arrives in China for high-stakes talks with Xi
stories.summary: The US president says his first request to Xi would be to "open up" China, while Beijing is expected to press Trump on the status of Taiwan.

stories.title: Syria needs 'comprehensive security agreement' with Israel, foreign minister tells Euronews
stories.summary: Al-Shaibani calls for a return to the 1974 security agreement between Israel and Syria and demands the withdrawal from territories seized. Remarks coincide with high-level political dialogue between Damascus and Brussels.

stories.title: Europe watches Beijing summit from the sidelines and fears the worst
stories.summary: The highly anticipated summit between US President Trump and his Chinese host Xi Jinping has begun - and Europe is watching from a distance. Yet, whatever the outcome is, there is little Brussels can be optimistic about.

stories.summary: Britain’s Catherine, Princess of Wales visited the Italian city of Reggio Emilia during her first official overseas trip since announcing her cancer remission.

stories.title: Cannes is a beautiful, gruelling circus. I wouldn’t quit it for anything | Agnès Poirier

After 25 years, I can’t help but go back Nothing prepares you for the shock that is the Cannes film festival: the adrenaline, the fatigue, the elation and the emotion, but also the hunger, the anger, the magic and the ridicule. For young cinephiles, and for almost everybody who works in the film industry, it is the mecca of cinema and has been so for nearly eight decades. Anyone going for the first time this week, as I

stories.title: Chinese court awards compensation to sacked worker replaced by AI
stories.summary: Case attracts widespread attention as example of China balancing enthusiastic adoption of AI with job security A court in China has ruled in favour of a worker whose company replaced him with artificial intelligence (AI), awarding him more than £28,000 in compensation. The worker, whose surname is Zhou, joined a tech company in the eastern city of Hangzhou in 2022 as a quality assurance supervisor overseeing large language models used in AI products. Continue reading...

stories.title: US man accused of killing rock singer in 1993 arrested after decades on the run
stories.summary: Richard Werstine, wanted in connection with killing of Cold as Life vocalist Rodney Barger, was arrested in Panama A suspect accused of murdering his rock singer friend in 1993 has been apprehended in Central America after spending more than 30 years successfully evading authorities. According to the US Marshals Service, Richard Werstine, who was wanted in connection with the killing of Cold as Life vocalist Rodney Barger, was arrested in Panama. Continue reading...

stories.title: Is caring about the climate unmanly?
stories.summary: The answer is complex — and says a lot about how masculinity works in the modern world.

stories.title: Satellites add a new layer to global poverty data
stories.summary: A new study using satellite data finds that 58% of the global population has been assigned to the wrong tier in the UN's Human Development Index. That can have real-world consequences for how aid reaches people.

stories.title: China’s new rules give the West a new headache
stories.summary: Beijing now has strong new powers to punish firms for pulling manufacturing out of China. Multinationals are increasingly caught in a web of coercion and complex rules as they try to operate across the US, EU and China.

stories.title: Ceasefire in sight? What's next for Russia's war in Ukraine
stories.summary: Four years since Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine, is the end finally in sight? DW spoke to US political experts about why they think US home elections could bring about a ceasefire.

stories.title: Cem Özdemir, first German state premier with Turkish roots
stories.summary: Cem Özdemir is the first leader of a German federal state to have Turkish roots. His Green Party won elections in the wealthy southwestern region of Baden-Württemberg.

stories.title: Israeli-Palestinian conflict: Allegations of sexual abuse
stories.summary: The issue of systemic sexual abuses as part of the Israeli-Palestinian conflict is back in the spotlight following new testimonies. Both Israel and Hamas deny the claims.

stories.title: Tigray tensions raise fears of Ethiopia-Eritrea war
stories.summary: The TPLF is reasserting control in Tigray after electing Debretsion Gebremichael. Rising tensions with Ethiopia's federal government are fueling fears of renewed conflict in the Horn of Africa.

stories.summary: Chinese robotics company Unitree Robotics unveiled a transformable humanoid “mecha” robot featuring a cockpit for a human pilot.

stories.title: Barcelona star Lamine Yamal waves Palestinian flag during La Liga celebrations

stories.title: Russian cargo ship sunk off Spain carried nuclear reactors for North Korea, probe reveals

stories.summary: Film fans gather overnight at Cannes, using stepladders and chains to secure red carpet spots as they wait for a glimpse of opening night stars.

stories.title: EU invites Taliban members to discuss Afghan migrant returns
stories.summary: The European Commission has invited officials from Afghanistan's Taliban to discuss the returns of certain migrants. It's controversial on humanitarian grounds, and because Brussels doesn't recognize Taliban authority.

stories.summary: On Sunday, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Ukraine would retaliate if Russia decided "to return to a full-scale war."

stories.summary: Tens of thousands of protesters marched through Brussels during a national strike called by Belgian unions against the government’s fiscal reforms.

It was born of the realisation that US leverage has slipped Friedrich Merz’s criticism of Donald Trump last month reflected more than a moment of personal candour or a split between Berlin and the White House. It pointed to a broader shift under way among European leaders. Increasingly they are willing to publicly confront the Trump administration on issues ranging from Iran to Ukraine and European sovereignty. The Trump administ

stories.title: Internal displacements caused by violence or conflict at record high in 2025

stories.title: Rendszerváltást a közlekedésben is!
stories.summary: A politika után a közlekedésben is rendszerváltásra van szükség, mert az elmúlt évtizedek közlekedéspolitikája nem a közjót, hanem az autó uralmát szolgálta.

stories.title: Belső vizsgálat indul, miután Sulyok Tamás államfő egyik MTI-képen sem látszik a miniszteri kinevezésen
stories.summary: A Duna Médiaszolgáltató azt akarja tudni, hogy sérültek-e a hírügynökségi fotózás szakmai szabályai.

stories.title: Felmondott Élő Gábor, a Hirado.hu főszerkesztője
stories.summary: A Telex kérdésére az MTVA megerősítette, hogy Élő munkaviszonya megszűnt, május 8-a volt az utolsó napja.

stories.title: EU negotiators agree new rules on vital medicines
stories.summary: A new agreement aims to tackle the drug shortages that have plagued the bloc. Among other things, it would make it easier to use public funds to support the production of essential medicines.

stories.title: What now for the role of high representative in Bosnia?
stories.summary: Christian Schmidt's departure as high representative in Bosnia and Herzegovina doesn't look like a routine diplomatic handover. It seems to indicate that international powers want something different from the role.

stories.title: Hijab-wearing German beauty contestant takes on the AfD
stories.summary: As a Miss Germany contestant, Büsra Sayed wore a hijab on stage. This had never happened before — and the far-right AfD didn't like it. Her response went viral.

stories.title: UK: Keir Starmer tells Cabinet he will continue in place, as four junior ministers quit
stories.summary: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has met with his Cabinet amid continued pressure after election losses. A set piece speech yesterday failed to calm the waters, and four junior ministers resigned on Tuesday.

stories.title: Hotter 2026 and El Nino could trigger extreme fires
stories.summary: More than 150 million hectares — over twice the size of Texas — burned globally in the first months of 2026. With a high chance of a supercharged El Nino, the second half of the year could be even worse.

stories.title: Iranian chief negotiator says US must accept peace proposal or face 'failure'
stories.summary: Iran has refused to back down in its war with Washington, with military officials warning they are prepared to respond to any renewed US attack.

stories.title: German ultrarunner Arda Saatçi completes 600-kilometre Death Valley to Los Angeles challenge
stories.summary: German athlete Arda Saatçi completed a 600-kilometre ultramarathon challenge from Death Valley to Los Angeles after running for more than 123 hours.

stories.title: Final passengers disembark hantavirus-hit cruise ship in Tenerife
stories.summary: The final passengers aboard the hantavirus-hit cruise ship MV Hondius disembarked in Tenerife before boarding flights to quarantine facilities in more than 20 countries.

stories.title: Macron thanks Kipchoge for 'patience' after Nairobi morning run
stories.summary: Macron joined marathon legend Eliud Kipchoge for an early morning run in Nairobi on day three of his Africa tour, later thanking the two-time Olympic champion for his "patience."

stories.title: At least nine killed after bomb rigged to rickshaw explodes at north Pakistan bazaar
stories.summary: The Pakistani Taliban and other militant groups have grown more emboldened since the Afghan Taliban returned to power in Kabul in 2021.

stories.title: Iran threatens weapons-grade uranium enrichment as peace talks falter
stories.summary: Tehran threatened to enrich uranium to weapons-grade if attacked again, as Trump said the ceasefire had a "one percent chance" of surviving after rejecting Tehran's proposal.

stories.title: Qatar warns Iran against weaponising Hormuz to 'blackmail' Gulf countries
stories.summary: Qatar warned Tehran on Tuesday against using the Strait of Hormuz as a "weapon" to hold Gulf states to ransom, as Doha and Ankara backed ongoing mediation efforts to end the Iran war and restore access to the vital waterway.

stories.title: Chinese people divided ahead of Trump-Xi summit in Beijing
stories.summary: Residents in Beijing shared mixed opinions ahead of the upcoming meeting between US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping, with some hoping for improved ties and others expressing skepticism.

stories.title: Peacocks take over northern Italian beach town
stories.summary: More than 100 peacocks now roam Punta Marina in northern Italy, leaving residents searching for humane ways to manage the birds.

stories.title: Zelensky's ex-chief of staff in court as Ukraine corruption probe escalates
stories.summary: Andriy Yermak was named by Ukraine's two anti-corruption agencies as a suspect in a money-laundering scheme.

stories.title: No sign of larger hantavirus outbreak, says UN health agency
stories.summary: The situation could still change and there might be more confirmed cases, warns the head of the World Health Organization.

stories.title: Kazakhstan launches driverless Light Rail Transit system after 10 years of delays
stories.summary: After a decade of construction delays, Astana has launched its Light Rail Transit (LRT) system, featuring driverless overground trains serving 18 stations across the city.

stories.title: SOCAR acquisition of Italy’s Italiana Petroli finalised after EU clearance
stories.summary: SOCAR has completed its acquisition of Italiana Petroli after securing all regulatory approvals, marking a major expansion of its downstream presence in Italy and strengthening its position in Europe’s energy market.

stories.title: Palestinians gather to mark 78th anniversary of the Nakba and call for 'right of return'
stories.summary: Palestinians in the occupied West Bank and the Gaza Strip marked the 78th anniversary of the Nakba, a defining moment in Palestinian history dating back to 1948, when hundreds of thousands of people were forced to flee their homes, villages and land, becoming refugees.

stories.title: Lebanon says two paramedics among 13 killed in Israeli strikes
stories.summary: Lebanon's health ministry says the paramedics were carrying out a rescue mission in Nabatieh in response to an earlier attack that killed one person.

stories.title: Australia has some of the world's costliest homes. Will scrapping tax breaks help?
stories.summary: The government hopes reforms will help young people enter the market, while critics say it will stifle supply.

stories.title: A decade on, Trump returns to a stronger and more assertive China
stories.summary: Beijing is arguably the most powerful competitor the US has confronted in its history, one analyst says.

stories.title: Air India crisis deepens ahead of final Ahmedabad crash report
stories.summary: Air India faces a leadership vacuum and mounting financial losses as it struggles to recover from the crash.

stories.title: Jason Collins, NBA's first openly gay player, dies aged 47
stories.summary: Collins shared late last year that he had been diagnosed with an aggressive form of brain cancer.

stories.title: Trump's 'Golden Dome' will cost $1.2tn and might not stop all-out missile attack
stories.summary: That pricetag is nearly seven times higher than Trump's initial estimate, found an independent budget office.

stories.title: Europe should behave more like China does if it wants to survive this age of chaos
stories.summary: Instead of obsessing over rules that have ceased to matter, we must consider giving Beijing a dose of its own medicine The US and Israel may have started the war in Iran, but – apart from the belligerents themselves – it is China and Europe that stand to lose the most from it. Yet while European leaders watch like rabbits caught in the headlights as energy prices shoot through the roof, China has responded to the crisis with remarkable equanimity. It is striking how self-confident Beijing is ahe

stories.title: EU preparing to offer key concession to UK in new post-Brexit agricultural deal
stories.summary: Exclusive: Britain expected to be allowed to keep ban on live animal exports, sources say, in fillip for Keir Starmer UK politics live – latest updates Brussels is preparing to offer Keir Starmer a key concession in talks over an agricultural deal, giving the beleaguered prime minister an important victory in his efforts to move closer to the EU. European officials have conceded that the UK can keep its ban on live animal exports as part of any joint deal on food and agricultural products, accor

stories.title: Germany news: 1-in-6 retailers fear for future
stories.summary: About one-in-12 German businesses worry about survival, with twice as many retailers jittery. Meanwhile in Stuttgart, five pro-Palestinian activists face trial over attacking an Israeli defense company site. Follow DW.

stories.title: French farmers dump manure at TotalEnergies refinery in fuel price protest
stories.summary: French farmers have staged a protest outside a major oil refinery near Lyon, dumping manure and rubbish at the entrance in anger over rising fuel costs and falling incomes.

stories.title: Sudan drone strikes killed at least 880 civilians between January and April, UN says
stories.summary: Most of the civilian deaths attributed to drone strikes in the first three months of the year were recorded in the Kordofan region and Darfur, although the strikes were also increasingly spreading beyond both regions.

stories.title: Britain and Ireland’s wildflowers – in pictures
stories.summary: The Eden Project’s National Wildflower Centre is opening entries for its Wildflower Photographer of the Year 2026 competition on 29 May. The contest showcases images of some of Britain and Ireland’s 1,600 wildflower species, and a selection of photos from last year’s competition will be on display at Eden Dock, Canary Wharf, London, during CWG’s Nature Week, from 13 July Continue reading...

stories.title: Weather tracker: US and Mexico brace for heatwave as deadly floods hit South Africa
stories.summary: Temperatures soar in California and Arizona, while deluge continues across Western and Northern Cape Heat is expected to intensify across western parts of the US and Mexico this week as a ridge of high pressure pushes temperatures well above the seasonal norm. Daytime highs are forecast to reach 10-15C above average in some areas. The US National Weather Service has issued heat advisories for parts of California and Arizona, with extreme heat warnings in force on Monday and Tuesday in places suc

stories.title: Why is Hollywood missing from Cannes?
stories.summary: There are no US blockbusters premiering at the Cannes Film Festival this year. So who's snubbing who?

stories.title: Germany's businesses frustrated by economic decline
stories.summary: No one had higher hopes for Friedrich Merz's chancellorship than the German business community. Now, however, early euphoria has given way to bitter disappointment.

stories.title: Somali piracy disrupts global shipping and trade routes
stories.summary: As ships reroute around Africa to avoid Middle East conflict zones, piracy off Somalia is back. The resurgence is driving up insurance, transit times and security costs, putting new pressure on global supply chains.

stories.title: Endemic, epidemic, pandemic: What's the difference?
stories.summary: The Andes hantavirus outbreak shows how easily disease labels are misunderstood — and why they describe patterns of spread, not how dangerous a virus is.

stories.title: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer 'not walking away' after local election mauling
stories.summary: Keir Starmer has said he takes "responsibility" for Labour's poor election performance, but also for putting matters right again. "I know I have my doubters and I know I need to prove them wrong, and I will," he said.

stories.title: Hantavirus: How it differs from COVID
stories.summary: In 2018-2019, the Andes virus hantavirus spread person-to-person in Argentina. Eleven people died — but it was stopped. Analysis of that outbreak shows the current one can also be stopped.

stories.title: Architecture of remembrance and hope: Daniel Libeskind at 80
stories.summary: Famous for designing the Jewish Museum Berlin and the Ground Zero project in New York, the architect has created dozens of memorable buildings worldwide.

stories.title: Hantavirus: How is the outbreak being contained?
stories.summary: Passengers evacuated from the MV Hondius cruise ship are returning to their home countries, some with symptoms, many without. What happens to these people and what is being done to contain the spread?
